name: Create a Next.js App Router Project from Scratch
description: Scaffold a new Next.js project with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and ESLint fully configured and ready for development.
trendingReason: Next.js App Router is the recommended architecture for new React projects in 2026

steps.title: Create the Next.js project
steps.description: Run create-next-app with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind, and ESLint enabled.
steps.tip: Replace --use-pnpm with --use-npm or --use-yarn based on your package manager preference.

steps.title: Navigate into the project and start the dev server
steps.description: Move into the project directory and verify everything works.

steps.title: Review the project structure
steps.description: Inspect the App Router file structure with the src/app directory layout.
steps.tip: layout.tsx is the root layout, page.tsx is the home page, and globals.css contains Tailwind directives.

steps.title: Add a new route
steps.description: Create a new page by adding a directory and page.tsx inside the app folder.
steps.command:
mkdir -p src/app/about && echo 'export default function AboutPage() { return

About

; }' > src/app/about/page.tsx

steps.title: Build for production
steps.description: Run a production build to verify there are no TypeScript or build errors.
steps.tip: Next.js will report any type errors, missing metadata, or page generation issues during build.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Next.js App Router Project Scaffold
set -euo pipefail

PROJECT_NAME=${1:-my-app}

echo "=== Creating Next.js App Router project: $PROJECT_NAME ==="
npx create-next-app@latest "$PROJECT_NAME" \
  --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --use-pnpm

cd "$PROJECT_NAME"

echo ""
echo "=== Installing additional dev dependencies ==="
pnpm add -D prettier prettier-plugin-tailwindcss

echo ""
echo "=== Creating Prettier config ==="
cat > .prettierrc.json << 'PRETTIEREOF'
{
  "semi": true,
  "singleQuote": false,
  "tabWidth": 2,
  "trailingComma": "es5",
  "plugins": ["prettier-plugin-tailwindcss"]
}
PRETTIEREOF

echo ""
echo "=== Project created successfully ==="
echo "cd $PROJECT_NAME && pnpm dev"

faq.question: Should I use App Router or Pages Router?
faq.answer: App Router is recommended for all new Next.js projects. It supports React Server Components, streaming, and parallel routes. Pages Router is stable but in maintenance mode.

faq.question: Do I need the src/ directory?
faq.answer: The src/ directory is optional but keeps your root clean by separating application code from config files like next.config.ts and tsconfig.json.

faq.question: Can I add other UI libraries like shadcn/ui later?
faq.answer: Yes. Run 'npx shadcn@latest init' after project creation. It auto-detects your Tailwind and TypeScript setup.
